title: An Online Approach for Entanglement Verification Using Classical Shadows
summary_bullets: The paper develops an online hybrid quantum–classical framework that processes classical shadows incrementally during the slack time between measurement shots, enabling real-time updates rather than offline post-processing.

Focusing on entanglement verification via PT-moments (trace moments of the partially transposed density matrix), the authors derive two unbiased online estimators that characterize the trade-off between memory footprint and per-shot computational cost: one memory-efficient estimator works directly from raw measurement records without constructing empirical density matrices, while the other maintains accumulated matrices to achieve constant per-shot cost and handle high moment orders.

By exploiting all combinations of snapshots and the incremental structure of classical shadows, the online estimators require fewer samples than state-of-the-art baselines in relevant regimes and turn PT-moment based entanglement detection from a purely offline diagnostic into a protocol that runs concurrently with experiments.
